mnemonic: A dish (几) with a single stroke — just one plain stroke: COMMONPLACE, ORDINARY, MEDIOCRE.
A heart (心) resting under a tongue and breath (舌+自+息) — your heart breathing a sigh beneath the tongue: RECESS, REST, RELAX, REPOSE.
A woman (女) as graceful as a flowing robe (爰) — a graceful woman gliding like silk: BEAUTIFUL WOMAN, PRINCESS.
Water (氵) in a purchased channel (冓) — water bought and directed into a cut channel: GUTTER, DITCH, SEWER, DRAIN.
Two hands (共) below a heart (心) — presenting your heart with both hands raised: RESPECT, REVERENT deference.
A blade (刂) cutting through stalks — a blade sweeping through the field: REAP, CUT, CLIP, TRIM, PRUNE.
An eye (目) hanging down like a feather (垂) — the eye drooping like a willow: DROWSY, SLEEP, the eye that DIES into slumber.
Metal (金) and an elder (昔/咋) colliding — old metal tangled with the new: CONFUSED, MIX, BE IN DISORDER.
A person (亻) and white (白) — the chief in white: CHIEF, COUNT, EARL, UNCLE — the first-born who stands apart.
Bamboo (竹) at the base — small slender BAMBOO GRASS, a native Japanese plant with no Chinese reading.
Grain (禾) with a shell (殳) — grain protected in a hard shell: CEREALS, GRAIN, the staple of civilization.
A hill (阝) with a pattern of earth ascending (夌) — a great earthen mound rising steeply: MAUSOLEUM, IMPERIAL TOMB, a burial MOUND.
Rain (雨) and a thicket of grass and affairs (務) — rain tangled in the air like unfinished business: FOG, MIST.
A cloud (云) drifting away from a ghost (鬼) — the ghost losing its shape and floating free: SOUL, SPIRIT.
Striking down (攴) something worn (敝) — beating something until it breaks: ABUSE, EVIL, VICE, BREAKAGE.
A woman (女) beside oneself (己/已) — a woman who stands as a self-contained power: QUEEN, PRINCESS.
A boat (舟) at a hundred (百) ports — a great ocean-going LINER, a massive SHIP.
Food (食) with an hungry ghost (我) — the ghost of hunger gnawing: STARVE, HUNGRY, THIRST.

meaning: hard up, destitute, suffer, perplexed, cornered
kunyomi: きわ.める, きわ.まる, きわ.まり, きわ.み
mnemonic: A person (人) trapped in a cave (穴) with a bent bow (弓) — cornered in a cave with no arrow left: HARD UP, DESTITUTE, CORNERED.

meaning: palm of hand, rule, administer, conduct
kunyomi: てのひら, たなごころ
mnemonic: A palm (手) under a business affair (尚) — the flat hand held up to RULE: PALM OF HAND, ADMINISTER, CONDUCT.

kunyomi: うるわ.しい, うら.らか
mnemonic: A deer-shaped crown over two linked figures — a pair of deer in full regalia: LOVELY, BEAUTIFUL, GRACEFUL, RESPLENDENT.
Thread (糸) with a water-like pattern (夌) — threads woven in flowing water patterns: DESIGN, FIGURED CLOTH, TWILL.

meaning: stinking, ill-smelling, suspicious looking, odor, savor, fragrance, be fragrant, stink, glow, be bright
kunyomi: くさ.い, -くさ.い, にお.う, にお.い
mnemonic: A self (自/nose) above a big dog (犬) — a dog sniffing its own nose, picking up every odor: STINKING, ILL-SMELLING, ODOR.

kunyomi: よろこ.ぶ, よろこ.ばす
mnemonic: A heart (忄) unlocked by a key (兌) — the key clicks and the heart opens wide: ECSTASY, JOY, RAPTURE.

kunyomi: は, やいば, き.る
mnemonic: A sword (刀) with a tiny dot marking the EDGE — that single dot is the sharp point: BLADE, SWORD, EDGE.
Thread (糸) binding a spirit (博 root) — threads wound tightly around you: TRUSS, ARREST, BIND, TIE, RESTRAIN.
The sun (日) hanging above a cliff with grain (厂+禾) — the sun marking grain harvests on a cliff wall: CALENDAR, ALMANAC.

kunyomi: よろ.しい, よろ.しく
mnemonic: Under a roof (宀), something properly placed (且) — everything in its right place under the roof: BEST REGARDS, GOOD, as it should be.
Death or loss (亡) over an eye (目) — the eye is gone, lost: BLIND, BLIND MAN, IGNORAMUS.

meaning: chic, style, purity, essence, pith, cream, elite, choice
mnemonic: Rice (米) with a samurai spirit (卒/粋) — the refined essence distilled like polished rice: CHIC, STYLE, PURITY, ESSENCE, ELITE.
A hand on the neck (辰+寸) — pressing down on someone's neck in shame: EMBARRASS, HUMILIATE, SHAME.
A boar (豕) struck with force (殳) and resolve — slaying a boar takes courage: STRONG, brave and bold.
A wheel (車) with a wedge (害) — the wedge that locks the wheel in place: CONTROL, WEDGE.
A dog (犭) in a garden (袁/えん sounds like 猿=monkey) — the dog-faced creature in the garden: MONKEY.
A bow (弓) with a thread stretched (玄) — the dark taut string of a bow: BOWSTRING, CHORD, HYPOTENUSE.

onyomi: ネン, ジン, ニン
mnemonic: Grain (禾) with a heart knowing (念) — grain that knows when to ripen: HARVEST, RIPEN, grow to fullness.
A cave (穴) stuffed with a clog (至) — the hole stuffed to the brim: PLUG UP, OBSTRUCT.
Fire (火) with something blowing (欠/吹) — blowing on fire to cook: COOK, BOIL the rice.
Water (氵) stretched wide like a giant (共) — water spread as far as giants can reach: DELUGE, FLOOD, VAST.

meaning: vicarious, surrogate, act in addition to, take in, absorb
kunyomi: おさ.める, かね.る, と.る
mnemonic: A hand (扌) gathering three ears (耳x3) — one hand pulling in three at once: VICARIOUS, ABSORB, TAKE IN.

kunyomi: あ.きる, あ.かす, あ.く
mnemonic: Food (食) wrapping itself (包) — food folded over and over, more than enough: SATED, TIRED OF, BORED, SATIATE.
A person (人) under a covered frame (冖) that is unnecessary — an extra person hiding under a useless cover: SUPERFLUOUS, USELESS.
A tree (木) with a sign of escape (兆) — the tree you run to in the peach garden: PEACH, soft and sweet.
A dog (犭) guarding (守) — the guard-dog let loose to track prey: HUNT, RAID, GATHER.
A tree (木) with one stroke through the trunk — marking the red heartwood: VERMILION, CINNABAR, SCARLET, RED.
Water (氵) in a pot (咼) spinning — water spinning in a pot: WHIRLPOOL, EDDY, VORTEX.
Thread (糸) tied to a spirit (申) — the long ceremonial belt tied to a divine sash: SIRE, GOOD BELT, GENTLEMAN.

meaning: hinge, pivot, door, center of things
mnemonic: A tree (木) at the center (区) — the central pivot of a wooden door: HINGE, PIVOT, the CENTER OF THINGS.
A stone (石) with a lowly servant (卑) — a stone carved to commemorate the low and the high: TOMBSTONE, MONUMENT.
Metal (金) beaten over steps (段) — hammering metal over and over on an anvil: FORGE, DISCIPLINE, TRAIN.
The curved shape of a blade — one simple arc: SWORD, SABER, KNIFE.
A drum (壴) struck by a hand (支) — the hand beating the drum: DRUM, BEAT, ROUSE, MUSTER.
Cloth (衣) stripped to fruit (果) — clothing stripped away, bare as fruit: NAKED, NUDE, UNCOVERED.
A dog-like creature (犭) still hesitating (酋) — the dog that pauses at the door: FURTHERMORE, STILL, YET undecided.

kunyomi: かたまり, つちくれ
mnemonic: Earth (土) with a ghost lump (鬼) — a mound of earth as big as a ghost: CLOD, LUMP, CHUNK, CLOT, MASS.
A banner (方) spinning on a foot (疋) — a flag spinning on a pole: ROTATION, GO AROUND.
The simple curved shape of a bow — just one elegant arc: BOW, BOW (archery, violin).

meaning: cash, bad habit, humble prefix, gift, Shinto offerings of cloth, rope, cut paper
mnemonic: Cloth (巾) beaten flat (敝) — cloth struck and offered: CASH, GIFT, SHINTO OFFERINGS of cloth and cut paper.
Flesh (月/肉) and a spreading cloth (莫) — flesh wrapped in a thin spreading layer: MEMBRANE.
A door (戸) held open by feathers (羽) — a feathered door fanning the air: FAN, FOLDING FAN.
Flesh (月) with a long rising sun (昜) — organs long and wound like the sun's rays: INTESTINES, GUTS, BOWELS.
Wood (木) framing a meeting place (曹) — wooden planks forming a container: VAT, TUB, TANK.
A slow growth (兹) rooted in the heart (心) — love growing slowly from deep in the heart: MERCY.
A tree (木) raised high (昜) — a tree reaching up to the sun: WILLOW, arms raised like sunbeams.

onyomi: バツ, ハツ, カ, ボチ
kunyomi: き.る, そむ.く, う.つ
mnemonic: A person (亻) struck with a weapon (戈) — the person is hit: FELL, STRIKE, ATTACK, PUNISH.

meaning: a good horse, speed, a fast person
mnemonic: A horse (馬) that is noble (夋) — a noble, swift horse striding ahead: A GOOD HORSE, SPEED, a FAST PERSON.

kunyomi: つ.ける, つ.かる, -づ.け, -づけ
mnemonic: Water (氵) seeping into everything (責) — liquid pressing into every fiber: PICKLING, SOAK, MOISTEN, STEEP.
Thread (糸) twisted tight (丩) — threads twisted together until they speak: TWIST, INVESTIGATE, VERIFY.
A person (亻) lit by the bright sky (京) — lit up from above, everything visible: CLEAR, HELP shed light.
Earth (土) piled over a large mound (賁) — earth heaped high in burial: TOMB, MOUND.

meaning: two-mat area, approx. thirty-six sq ft
mnemonic: Earth (土) measured flat (平) — flat earth measured out: TWO-MAT AREA, approx. thirty-six sq ft.
Thread (糸) dyed deep blue (甘+감) — silk steeped in deep blue dye: DARK BLUE, NAVY.

meaning: disconcerted, be confused, lose one's head
kunyomi: あわ.てる, あわ.ただしい
mnemonic: A heart (忄) turning wild (荒) — a heart going wild like a wasteland: DISCONCERTED, CONFUSED, LOSE ONE'S HEAD.
A woman (女) entertained by a king (吾/呉) — a woman delighted at the king's entertainment: RECREATION, PLEASURE.
A mouth (口) over five (五) — speaking as one among five: I, MY, OUR, ONE'S OWN.
A tree (木) in spring (春) — a tree that blooms in spring: CAMELLIA, the spring flower.
A mouth (口) with something inside it — that thing in the mouth is the TONGUE, the reed's CLAPPER.

meaning: gauze, thin silk, Rome, arrange, spread out
mnemonic: Net (罒) spread over silk (維) — a net of thin silk spread wide: GAUZE, THIN SILK, ROME, SPREAD OUT.
A mountain (山) with two peaks (夾) squeezed together — two mountain walls squeezing in: GORGE, RAVINE.
A person (亻) given an offering (奉) — the person receives the dedicated gift: STIPEND, SALARY.
A cliff (厂) with a tiny village (里) — a tiny village under a cliff, worth almost nothing: RIN, 1/10 sen, 1/10 bu.
A mountain (山) with a bee's peak (峯/逢) — the sharp summit where only bees dare fly: SUMMIT, PEAK.
Two squares of soil (土土) stacked — a jade gem shaped like stacked earth: SQUARE JEWEL, CORNER, ANGLE, EDGE.
A sake jar (酉) with a long process (壌) — letting it ferment for a long long time: BREW, CAUSE a reaction.
Grass (艹) connecting (連) everything — plants linked together on the water: LOTUS.

kunyomi: とむら.う, とぶら.う
mnemonic: A bow (弓) with something hanging down — the ribbon of mourning hanging from a bow: CONDOLENCES, MOURNING, FUNERAL.

meaning: the latter, duplicate, strange, witty, fishhook radical (no. 5)
mnemonic: A fishhook curve — the second item on a list, the one that bends: THE LATTER, DUPLICATE, STRANGE, WITTY.

meaning: soup, juice, broth, sap, gravy, pus
mnemonic: Water (氵) dripping from a ten (十) — ten drops falling from a ladle: SOUP, JUICE, BROTH, SAP, GRAVY.
A body (尸) over a hidden person (匕) — a robed figure crouching in devotion: NUN.
Walking (辶) flat everywhere (扁) — walking flatly in every direction: EVERYWHERE, WIDELY, GENERALLY.
A scale (行) balanced with a horn (角) and fish (魚) — a fish and a horn perfectly balanced: EQUILIBRIUM, MEASURING ROD, SCALE.

meaning: send forth fragrance, fragrant, be scented, smoke (tobacco)
mnemonic: Grass/plants (艹) sending up black smoke (熏) — plants releasing their fragrance like smoke: FRAGRANT, SCENTED, send forth FRAGRANCE.
A dog (犭) tracking through leaves (猟) — the hunting dog tracking through the forest: GAME-HUNTING, SHOOTING, GAME.
The horns, head, and four legs of a sheep — a simple, direct picture: SHEEP.
To strike (欠) with goodwill (㝵) — striking a bargain with a friendly tap: GOODWILL, ARTICLE, SECTION, FRIENDSHIP.
A gate (門) where someone inside inspects (兌) — standing at the gate checking everything through: REVIEW, INSPECTION, REVISION.
A person (亻) probing (貞) — a person quietly probing for truth: SPY.
A mouth (口) that is thirsty and dried out (曷) — a dry parched throat: HOARSE, SCOLD with a raspy voice.

meaning: daring, brave, bold, sad, tragic, pitiful
kunyomi: あ.えて, あ.えない, あ.えず
mnemonic: Striking (攴) a wild boar (㪐) head-on — charging the boar without flinching: DARING, BRAVE, BOLD.
Flesh (月) shaped like a platform (台) — flesh forming a cradle-platform: WOMB, UTERUS.
Sake (酉) with filial piety (孝) — the dutiful fermentation that honors tradition: FERMENTATION.
A heart (忄) overflowing like a flood (賁) — a heart bursting its banks: AROUSED, RESENTFUL, INDIGNANT, ANGRY.
Pork (月/肉) with a pig-like creature (豕) — flesh of the swine: PORK, PIG.
Walking (辶) with something blocking (庶) — something blocking your path as you walk: INTERCEPT, INTERRUPT, OBSTRUCT.

meaning: front door, title page, front page
mnemonic: A door (戸) with a non (非) — a door that says no, standing firm: FRONT DOOR, TITLE PAGE, FRONT PAGE.
Stone (石) with a flowing pattern (流) — a stone that flows like a river when burned: SULPHUR.
Walk/strike (赤/攴) with forgiveness (舍) — the authority strikes and then forgives: PARDON, FORGIVENESS.
A cave (穴) entered by stealth (切) — cutting quietly into the cave: STEALTH, STEAL, SECRET, PRIVATE, HUSHED.
Water (氵) wrapped up (包) — water wrapped in a thin skin: BUBBLES, FOAM, SUDS, FROTH.
A jewel (王/玉) with lucky water (端/沚) — a gem glowing with auspicious dew: CONGRATULATIONS, a lucky SIGN.

meaning: or again, furthermore, on the other hand
kunyomi: また, また-, また.の-
mnemonic: The character looks like a hand reaching out AGAIN — a forked shape that says 'on the other hand, OR AGAIN, try once more.'
A heart (忄) that LAMENTS so deeply it sighs into the air — imagine a grieving man pressing his chest in SAD rue.
Silk threads (糸) wrapped around a square shape — a spindle SPINNING yarn round and round into cloth.

meaning: regret, bear a grudge, resentment, malice, hatred
kunyomi: うら.む, うら.めしい
mnemonic: A heart (忄) that keeps a grudge like a root (艮) dug deep into the soil — RESENTMENT that never lets go.
Flesh (月) that expands outward like a square room — body FAT that fills every corner, making you OBESE.
A hand (扌) supporting a man leaning left — reach out and AID your friend before he falls.

kunyomi: たわむ.れる, ざ.れる, じゃ.れる
mnemonic: A tiger and a halberd beside a virtual (虚) space — warriors FROLICKING in PLAY, sword-dancing for sport.
A person (人) standing beside the number five (五) in a LINE — soldiers in a FIVE-MAN SQUAD filing past.

kunyomi: い.む, い.み, い.まわしい
mnemonic: A snake coiling above the heart (心) — the sight fills you with ABHORRENCE; you MOURN and DETESTABLE dread.
Water (氵) that has become MURKY — a river swirling with mud, UNCLEAN and full of IMPURITY.
Three men (大) running at full sprint — picture a frantic BUSTLE, everyone RUNNING in wild chaos.

meaning: Big Dipper, ten sho (vol), sake dipper, dots and cross radical (no. 68)
mnemonic: The BIG DIPPER constellation drawn as a ladle scooping the night sky — a ten-sho SAKE DIPPER under the stars.
Flowers (艹) inside a grand gate — elegant ORCHIDS framed like the gates of HOLLAND's tulip fields.
A lightning bolt inside the movement radical (辶) — something so SWIFT and FAST it leaves a trail.
A small (小) piece of flesh (月) — a chip off the old block, a perfect RESEMBLANCE to the original.
A metal (金) BOWL used as a crown — monks carry their rice-tub POT like a CROWN on their heads.
A tree (木) growing through a mortar (臼) that crumbles — wood left to DECAY and ROT in seclusion.

onyomi: カク, コク, バイ
mnemonic: The husk left after an ugly beating — a NUT SHELL, the empty HUSK that remains after life departs.

meaning: enjoy, receive, undergo, answer (phone), take, get, catch
mnemonic: A lid placed over a mouth on a pedestal — to ENJOY and RECEIVE gifts offered up on an altar.

meaning: Manchu dynasty, name given to naturalized foreigners
mnemonic: Grain (禾) harvested by hands under a roof — the MANCHU DYNASTY built on harvested land and naturalized foreigners.
Grass (艹) cut with a lance — wild MISCANTHUS REED slashed to make thatched roofs in the countryside.
Tall plants (艹) enclosing water and a boundary — a CLAN walled inside its ENCLOSURE, a feudal domain.
Water (氵) and a few (少) drops — where water barely trickles, it leaves behind SAND.
A wheel (車) with a side-rail bracket — the extra rod that braces the wheel and HELPS and AIDS its load.
A woman (女) who stands between two trees — the go-between MEDIATOR who arranges marriages.
A bird (鳥) by a household — the humble CHICKEN that rules every farmyard, clucking in its coop.
A single altar (示) in a quiet grove — sit cross-legged under the trees in SILENT MEDITATION, ZEN.

kunyomi: しょく.する, たの.む
mnemonic: A mouth (口) with a long reach (属) — ENTRUST a message by speaking it at length, REQUEST delivery.

meaning: trunk, torso, hull (ship), hub of wheel
mnemonic: The same (同) flesh (月) — the shared midsection, the TRUNK and TORSO that every body holds in common.
Moving along (辶) in turns — messengers ALTERNATING on a relay route, TRANSFERRING the baton.
A hand (扌) pushing something between — INSERTING a sword into a scabbard, PUT IN between two layers.
Wind (風) over a mountain (山) — a mountain STORM, a raging TEMPEST tearing down the slopes.
A tree (木) used as a blunt mallet — the SPINE's vertebrae stacked like a MALLET's heavy head.
Silk thread (糸) woven in the finest manner — pure lustrous SILK, soft as a whisper.
Climbing the mound (阜) to stand beside — a loyal attendant who ACCOMPANIES and FOLLOWS at every step.
A knife (刂) cutting a block in half — slicing with precision to DIVIDE the whole into parts.

meaning: musical score, music, note, staff, table, genealogy
mnemonic: Words (言) spread across a father-like staff — notes arranged on a page, a MUSICAL SCORE mapping sound.
The fragrance (有) of a town (邑) — CULTURAL PROGRESS and PERFUME wafting from a refined city.
A heart (心) stretched far into the distance — PERMANENCE and LEISURE in DISTANT, long-stretching time.
Water (氵) and a pure elder — a GRACEFUL and GENTLE woman, pure as still water at dawn.
A cloth (巾) catching the wind — the billowing SAIL that drives a ship across the sea.
The sun (日) breaking through the dark (尭) — DAYBREAK and DAWN, the moment of sudden understanding.
A person (人) of supreme distinction — a tall figure who towers above all in GREATNESS and EXCELLENCE.

onyomi: ナン, ダン, ゼン, ネン
mnemonic: A tree (木) as warm as the south (南) — the fragrant CAMPHOR TREE whose wood repels insects.

meaning: flute, clarinet, pipe, whistle, bagpipe, piccolo
mnemonic: Bamboo (竹) scooped out to speak — blow air through the bamboo FLUTE and it sings.
A jewel (王) that makes a clear sound — SOUND OF JEWELS, a tinkling bell-like ringing.
A hand (又) grabbing a woman — a forced SLAVE or lowly FELLOW, a rough manservant.
A metal (金) device shaped like a fixed tablet — a hard metal LOCK holding the door fast in FETTERS.
Two hands folded under a roof — clench them together into a tight FIST, ready to strike.
Like wings (羽) on a sheep (羊) — imagine a flying lamb that SOARS high, taking FLIGHT.

kunyomi: うつ.る, うつ.す, みやこがえ
mnemonic: Moving along a path of many changes — a long TRANSITION and MOVE, shifting capital cities through time.
A hand (扌) that works out of (出) sync — CLUMSY and BUNGLING fingers that can't get it right.
A person (人) who WAITS at a temple — the faithful SAMURAI who WAITS UPON and SERVES his lord.

meaning: shaku, Japanese foot, measure, scale, rule
mnemonic: An elbow bent at a right angle — the distance from elbow to wrist, the Japanese SHAKU foot MEASURE.

meaning: mountain peak, mountain pass, climax, crest, (kokuji)
mnemonic: A mountain (山) with an up-down path — the MOUNTAIN PASS at the very CREST and CLIMAX of the trail.
Bamboo (竹) riding a horse — a FERVENT rider, sincerely CORDIAL and DELIBERATE in every step.

onyomi: チョウ, ジョウ, トウ
mnemonic: A hand hammering at the beginning — the very first strike that marks the BEGINNING of creation.
Water (氵) that has gone dry (乞) — desperate THIRST, a river that DRIES UP and PARCHES the land.
A hand picking up something small — a YOUTH who picks up after his elders, an UNCLE in training.
A bird with small (此) markings — the subtler, quieter FEMALE bird, the FEMININE of the species.

onyomi: コウ, キョウ, ホウ
mnemonic: A lid over a mouth on a base — a smooth road where everything PASSES THROUGH and GOES SMOOTHLY.

kunyomi: た.える, たま.る, こら.える, こた.える
mnemonic: Earth (土) that can WITHSTAND a pit of water — the ground that ENDURES flooding and RESISTS erosion.
Two mouths recounting events in order — to NARRATE and DESCRIBE, to CONFER a tale in sequence.
A wine jar (酉) that has gone sour — the sharp, puckering taste of VINEGAR and ACID.
A mouth (口) held tight (今) — VERSIFYING in a low hum, SINGING and reciting a poem softly.
Moving along (辶) by taking turns — a RELAY system where messengers pass the packet IN TURN.
A mountain (山) so tall it commands all — the sharp PEAK and SUMMIT that lords over the landscape.

kunyomi: はなは.だ, はなは.だしい
mnemonic: A man pressing very hard on a mat — TREMENDOUSLY forceful, EXCEEDINGLY great pressure.
A tall (高) mouth on stilts — someone BOASTING and HIGH-TALKING, proud to stand above the rest.
A mountain (山) that is worshipped — people gaze up to ADORE and REVERE the towering mountain.
Water (氵) and a tree that weeps black — the dark sap that LACQUERS and VARNISHES everything it touches.
A mountain (山) with a field (甲) jutting out — a narrow HEADLAND or CAPE stabbing into the sea.

meaning: mannerism, habit, vice, trait, fault, kink
mnemonic: A sickness (疒) that clings like a wall (辟) — a MANNERISM or HABIT so ingrained it feels like a disease.

kunyomi: たの.しい, たの.しむ
mnemonic: A heart (忄) that says yes (兪) — the heart jumping with PLEASURE and HAPPINESS, full of JOY.

meaning: sign of the tiger, 3-5AM, third sign of Chinese zodiac
mnemonic: A house (宀) with a tiger inside — the TIGER's hour at dawn, third zodiac sign prowling 3-5 AM.
A rock (石) burned to a REEF — sharp SUNKEN ROCKS just below the surface, a sailor's nightmare.

onyomi: ナイ, ダイ, ノ, アイ
kunyomi: の, すなわ.ち, なんじ
mnemonic: A sweeping curve like a vine — a flowing POSSESSIVE PARTICLE, whereupon and accordingly the vine extends.
Water (氵) surrounding a small island — a SANDBAR or CONTINENT rising from the water.
Soldiers gathered around a sprout — troops camping together in BARRACKS, a CAMP forming around a pole.
A tree (木) with a flower-red bark — the pale white BIRCH tree, its bark a DARK RED blush.
A tree (木) with a pointed crown — an ornamental EVERGREEN TWIG, always green and upright.
A woman (女) tied to a cause (因) — bound by MATRIMONY, two fates joined in MARRIAGE.

kunyomi: いわ, いわお, けわ.しい
mnemonic: A mountain (山) stern as a face — massive ROCK and CRAGGY BOULDERS, steep and forbidding.

meaning: mimic, aim (a gun) at, nominate, imitate
mnemonic: A hand (扌) copying another — MIMICKING and IMITATING, a hand that AIM-imitates another's gesture.
An earth-and-tile (土+瓦) barrier — a garden WALL or FENCE dividing properties.
A mouth framed by morning (辰) — the LIPS that part at dawn to speak the day's first word.

kunyomi: むつ.まじい, むつ.む, むつ.ぶ
mnemonic: An eye (目) full of warmth — FRIENDLY and INTIMATE gazes, eyes full of HARMONIOUS affection.
A tree (木) inside a gate (門) — nothing moves; peaceful LEISURE inside the garden gate.
Ancient flesh (月) with a foreign hat — a BARBARIAN and FOREIGN stranger from beyond the border.

meaning: seclude, confine to a room, deep, profound, secluded, faint, dark, tranquil, calm
kunyomi: ふか.い, かす.か, くら.い, しろ.い
mnemonic: Mountains faintly outlined in a dark hollow — SECLUDED and DEEP, FAINT and TRANQUIL in the darkness.
A mountain (山) that rises sharply — a HIGH and STEEP cliff, dangerously sharp at the summit.
Two books stacked inside a sun — clerks in the OFFICE copying documents, COMRADES at their desks.
Words (言) sung in a long tone (永) — RECITATION of a POEM, singing verses in an elegant SONG.

kunyomi: いや.しい, いや.しむ, いや.しめる
mnemonic: A field (田) pressed down to the ground — something LOW and BASE, VULGAR and LOWLY underfoot.

meaning: scorn, despise, make light of, contempt
kunyomi: あなど.る, あなず.る
mnemonic: A person (人) twisting another down — to SCORN and DESPISE, to MAKE LIGHT of someone.

onyomi: チュウ, イ, シュ, シュウ
mnemonic: Metal (金) poured into a mold (寿) — molten metal CAST and MINTED into a new shape.
A hand (扌) smoothing over the end (末) — RUB it clean, PAINT over it, ERASE the last trace.

meaning: military officer, jailer, old man, rank
mnemonic: A hand holding a fire-iron to an old man — a MILITARY OFFICER of old RANK, a jailer holding authority.
A tree (木) that stands like a rule (規) — the tall ZELKOVA TREE, straight and noble.
A slave following behind the mound (阜) — a SERVANT and PRISONER forced to FOLLOW as a SLAVE.
An altar (示) with a skull (咼) — a CALAMITY from the gods, MISFORTUNE and EVIL CURSE.
A butterfly (虫) fluttering through the leaves (枼) — delicate, dancing BUTTERFLY drifting on the breeze.

meaning: dairy products, whey, broth, fruit juice
mnemonic: A wine jar (酉) with white (白) milk — DAIRY PRODUCTS curdled in a jar, creamy WHEY and BROTH.
A plant (艹) with a backbone — the rigid STALK and STEM that holds the flower upright.
A cloth banner leading troops — the COMMANDER who holds the flag and LEADS TROOPS into battle.
Moving along (辶) a one-way path — to DEPART forever, to DIE and not return.
Water (氵) that rises into air (气) — boiling water releasing VAPOR and STEAM.
A gem (王) carved by a hand — to POLISH a raw stone until it shines with beauty.
Something hidden inside a container (匚) — HIDE and SHELTER a fugitive, SHIELD the secret within.

meaning: collar, neck, lapel, one's inner feelings
mnemonic: A garment (衤) fastened at the heart — the COLLAR and LAPEL that covers one's INNER FEELINGS.
An insect (虫) that glows with fire (火) — the FIREFLY, nature's tiny LIGHTNING-BUG lantern.
A plant (艹) burned by the scorching sun — the tropical BANANA and PLANTAIN, scorched and lush.
A lone person under a roof with many — outnumbered, a WIDOW left with a MINORITY, only a FEW.
A precious jewel (王) flowing like a river (流) — rare gems like LAPIS LAZULI streaming with color.
A sickness (疒) that flows uncontrolled — the uncomfortable, uncontrollable flow of DIARRHEA.
A building doing ordinary work — COMMONPLACE and ORDINARY employment, average and unremarkable.
Two moons side by side — two bright lights that shine together as COMPANIONS and FRIENDS.
An earth (土) that opens into a hollow — a dark PIT or HOLE dug into the ground.
Plants (艹) over a basket of dye — the deep, rich blue of INDIGO dye soaked from the plant.
Money (貝) seized by a warrior — a BURGLAR and ROBBER who steals with weapons, a TRAITOR rebel.
A hand (扌) pressing the narrow (窄) — SQUEEZE out the liquid, press tightly to extract every drop.

kunyomi: あぜ, くろ, ほとり
mnemonic: A rice field (田) split by a half (半) — the PADDY RIDGE or LEVEE that runs between the fields.
Moving along a path without end — the DISTANT horizon that retreats as you walk toward it.
A mouth (口) singing a ballad — a SONG or BALLAD sung from the depths of the heart.

meaning: cavity, hole, slit, very, great, exceedingly
mnemonic: A child (子) with a gap — a cavity or HOLE bored through, named after Confucius (Kǒng Zǐ).
A tree (木) holding a bright orange globe — the MANDARIN ORANGE tree glowing with citrus fruit.

onyomi: ソウ, シュウ, ス
kunyomi: くちすす.ぐ, くちそそ.ぐ, うがい, すす.ぐ
mnemonic: Water (氵) rushing through the teeth — GARGLE and RINSE MOUTH, water swirling and spitting clean.
Two mouths stacked like vertebrae — the SPINE and BACKBONE, segments stacked one on another.
A hand (扌) striking with force (考) — TORTURE and BEAT someone to extract information.
A woman (女) raised in grandeur (壌) — a pampered young GIRL, a Miss or DAUGHTER of fine breeding.
Plants (艹) enclosed for the soul — a GARDEN or PARK cultivated as a peaceful FARM retreat.
Two bowing figures under a frame — they bow to the SOUTHEAST, where the wind is said to originate.

kunyomi: もり, ふさ.ぐ, やまなし
mnemonic: A tree (木) with a soil mound (土) — a quiet GROVE or WOODS, a sacred FOREST of trees.
Water (氵) running through narrow land — a MOUNTAIN STREAM cutting through a narrow VALLEY.
Feathers and a public elder — a wise VENERABLE OLD MAN, feather-capped and respected.

meaning: bargain, reason, charge, suspicion, point, account, purity, honest, low price, cheap, rested, contented, peaceful
mnemonic: A house with sharp corners — the HONEST man who keeps things at low BARGAIN prices, CHEAP and pure.
Words (言) chosen with care (菫) — speaking DISCREETLY and REVERENTLY, HUMBLY weighing each syllable.
An eye (目) that is round as a child (童) — the dark, round PUPIL OF THE EYE gazing back at you.
Water (氵) rising from below like courage — a spring that BOILS up and SEETHES, UPROAR welling up.

onyomi: キン, ゴン, コン
kunyomi: よろこ.ぶ, よろこ.び
mnemonic: A person beside a yawn, smiling — to REJOICE and TAKE PLEASURE IN, grinning ear to ear.
A house (宀) with羊 and fire (火) — a dome-shaped KILN or OVEN baking clay into ceramic.
Garments (衣) wrapped in reward — to PRAISE and EXTOL someone, draping them in complimentary robes.
A wine jar (酉) with a ghost face — UGLY and UNCLEAN, a face that causes shame, BAD LOOKING.
A box marked with a cross — a MEASURING BOX holding exactly 1.8 liters of SAKE.
Following death (歹) into a river (旬) — MARTYRDOM, following one's lord by resigning into death.

meaning: anxiety, trouble, worry, pain, ill, annoy, nuisance, irksome
kunyomi: わずら.う, わずら.わす, うるさ.がる, うるさ.い
mnemonic: Fire (火) spreading through the head (頁) — ANXIETY and WORRY spreading like fire, an IRKSOME nuisance.
A coiled comma shape — the swirling COMMA-DESIGN of a magatama, a spiral WHIRLPOOL.
An altar (示) bearing good signs — HAPPINESS and GOOD FORTUNE shown at the shrine, AUSPICIOUS blessings.
A knife (刂) probing a crime — sharp legal CENSURE and CRIMINAL INVESTIGATION cutting into guilt.

kunyomi: お.ちる, くず.す, くず.れる
mnemonic: Earth (土) collapsing from a high place — to DEGENERATE and DESCEND TO ruin, to LAPSE INTO decay.
Grain (禾) given to the state — the harvest CROP TAX and TARIFF borrowed back by the people.
Grain (禾) with a ridgeline (夌) — the sharp EDGE and CORNER of power, projecting MAJESTY.
A tree (木) forming a crossing frame — a SCAFFOLD or CLEAT, a wooden JETTY bolting across a gap.
A person (人) who is gentle (委) — the ancient YAMATO spirit, graceful old JAPAN bowing in harmony.
A woman (女) with a man serving (胥) — the new BRIDEGROOM who serves the family, the SON-IN-LAW.

meaning: pining, yearn for, love dearly, adore
mnemonic: A heart (心) reaching up through the grass — YEARNING and PINING, the heart that LOVES DEARLY from afar.
A woven pattern of beauty — BEAUTIFUL and PATTERNED like fine brocade cloth.
A net (罒) over energy (能) — QUIT and STOP, withdraw when the net of exhaustion falls on you.

meaning: rectify, straighten, correct, reform, cure, control, pretend, falsify
mnemonic: An arrow (矢) bent straight (喬) — to RECTIFY and STRAIGHTEN, to CORRECT a bent or crooked thing.

meaning: so-and-so, one, a certain, that person
kunyomi: それがし, なにがし
mnemonic: A tree (木) growing in a field (甘) — SO-AND-SO, A CERTAIN unnamed person, that INDIVIDUAL.
A person (人) trapped inside a box (囗) — a CRIMINAL caught and locked in a cell, under ARREST.
A demon (鬼) out ahead — the frightening figure CHARGING AHEAD OF OTHERS, taking the lead.
An insect (虫) that spans the sky — the dragon-worm arching across as a RAINBOW after rain.

meaning: large bird, wild goose, large, great, powerful, prosperous
kunyomi: おおとり, ひしくい, おおがり
mnemonic: A bird (鳥) by a river (江) — a majestic WILD GOOSE, LARGE and powerful, a GREAT bird.

meaning: ooze, flow, soak in, penetrate, secrete
mnemonic: Water (氵) that seeps through a hidden (必) path — to OOZE and SECRETE, soaking in and penetrating slowly.

kunyomi: おい.て, お.ける, ああ, より
mnemonic: A square grid with a diagonal mark — AT and IN this location, ON this spot, AS FOR this point.
Walking (走) with strength (丩) — marching forward STRONG AND BRAVE, full of warrior vigor.

kunyomi: ようや.く, やや, ようよ.う, すす.む
mnemonic: Water (氵) chopping bit by bit (斬) — STEADILY and GRADUALLY advancing, FINALLY barely reaching the goal.
An insect (虫) that whines like a note (文) — the humming MOSQUITO whose tiny whine drives you mad.
Plants (艹) opening to the sun — the HOLLYHOCK reaching upward like outstretched hands.
A cliff (厂) with a kneeling figure — bad luck crouching under a cliff, UNLUCKY MISFORTUNE and DISASTER.
Plants (艹) floating in water — SEAWEED and DUCKWEED drifting on the current.
An altar (示) that records rewards — a PENSION and GRANT blessed by the gods, HAPPINESS in FIEF.

onyomi: モウ, ボウ, ミョウ
mnemonic: A child (子) in a dish — the CHIEF child Mencius who sat in a basin to learn, a BEGINNING of wisdom.
A woman (女) of the main line (商) — the LEGITIMATE WIFE, child of DIRECT DESCENT, no bastard.
A person raised HIGH — standing tall and FAR above the rest, elevated like Emperor Yao.
A fire-mouth (赫) and shout — to MENACE and THREATEN with a terrifying DIGNITY and roar.

meaning: sign of the snake or serpent, 9-11AM, sixth sign of Chinese zodiac
mnemonic: A coiled snake frozen in mid-curl — the SIGN OF THE SNAKE, 9-11 AM, sixth zodiac sign.
A shape that sticks out — clearly CONVEX, jutting outward, the opposite of concave.
The sun (日) and an unblocked channel (申) — light flowing without obstruction, free to STRETCH.
Words (言) that circle back to the same sound (員) — RHYME and ELEGANCE, the repeating TONE of poetry.
Rain (雨) that falls as white crystal (相) — the biting white FROST that coats the ground at dawn.
Stone (石) that burns away (肖) — NITRATE and SALTPETER, the mineral that vanishes in fire.

kunyomi: いまし.める, みことのり
mnemonic: A bundle (束) of force (力) from above — an IMPERIAL ORDER delivered with royal authority.
Grass (艹) growing near a well (斤) — the fresh green PARSLEY sprouting by the water's edge.

onyomi: キョウ, アン, コウ
mnemonic: A tree (木) with an opening mouth — the sweet APRICOT whose pit splits open like a grin.
A wooden (木) chamber sealed like a building (官) — the COFFIN and CASKET that holds the departed.
A person (人) who needs (需) rain to grow — a CONFUCIAN scholar, patient and learned as a rooted tree.
A bird (鳥) of mythic proportion — the magnificent MALE MYTHICAL BIRD, the phoenix of legends.
A fragrant sound drifting from the altar (殸) — FRAGRANT and BALMY, a FAVOURABLE scent carried on the air.
A heart (心) that sweeps clean (彗) — the broom of wisdom clearing the mind, making you WISE.
Autumn (秋) pressing on the heart (心) — the melancholy weight of fall, DISTRESS and GRIEF.
A tree-tower (木) that stands on a slope (婁) — a tall WATCHTOWER or HIGH BUILDING rising above the city.

kunyomi: うるわ.しい, あき.らか
mnemonic: Two trees side by side (林) refined by patterns (彡) — REFINED and GENTLE, radiating quiet elegance.
A frame (匚) with a king (王) inside — a square container that CORRECTS and ASSISTS, SAVING the shape.
An eye (目) under grass fringe — the bushy strip of hair above the eye, the EYEBROW.
A gold (金) person bowing their mouth — to RESPECT and REVERE with a bow, LONGING for something noble.
Grass (艹) and firewood (斤+木) — bundles of FIREWOOD and KINDLING gathered for FUEL.
Garments (衤) of dull brown (曷) — rough BROWN woollen cloth, a plain kimono of WOOLLEN BROWN.

kunyomi: たまわ.る, たま.う, たも.う
mnemonic: Money (貝) given by the emperor (易) — a royal GRANT and GIFT, a BOON bestowed from above.
The mountain 山 looms STEEP and CRAGGY — its jagged peak slashes the sky like a serrated blade.
Threads of silk 糸 woven through a palm 宗 — to RULE and SYNTHESIZE all strands into one unified cloth.

meaning: darning, repair, mend, trim, tidy up, adjust
mnemonic: You carefully work silk threads 糸 through a torn garment, the needle making a 善 (good) pattern — to DARN and REPAIR with virtuous hands.
A tree 木 trunk shaped into a perfect cylinder — the wooden PLUG or CORK that BOLTS a barrel shut.
Feathers 羽 shimmer with the emerald hue of water — the KINGFISHER's brilliant GREEN plumage catching the light.
The fish 魚 slips upstream through electric currents 占 — the FRESHWATER TROUT leaping over rocks at dawn.
A tree 木 stands TRUE 真 in the forest — the HAZELNUT tree dropping its treasure to the ground each autumn.

kunyomi: くぼ.む, へこ.む, ぼこ
mnemonic: The shape itself tells the story — two walls rising with a hollow between them: perfectly CONCAVE, SUNKEN inward.

meaning: glossy, luster, glaze, polish, charm, colorful, captivating
kunyomi: つや, なま.めかしい, あで.やか, つや.めく, なま.めく
mnemonic: A woman 豊 (abundant) whose rich curves GLEAM like polished lacquer — her GLOSSY CHARM is CAPTIVATING, radiant with luster.
The heart 忄 feels ALL 総 things at once — EVERYTHING flows through this single, all-encompassing heart.
Vines of grass 艹 with a bird 鳥 hidden inside — IVY clings to the wall the way a bird clings to a branch.
Metal 金 is kneaded 柬 repeatedly in fire — to TEMPER and REFINE steel until it gleams with disciplined brilliance.
A bird 隹 plunges from the sky with lethal speed — the FALCON, nature's perfect hunting missile.
Water 氵 meets land at a quiet place 者 — the STRAND where waves whisper against a lonely SHORE.
The heart 心 wrapped inside clothing 衣 — the INMOST feelings hidden deep within, safe in the chest.

meaning: pursue, drive away, chase, accomplish, attain, commit
mnemonic: A pig 豕 chased down a road 辶 — to PURSUE, to DRIVE AWAY, to CHASE until the task is accomplished.

meaning: reject, retreat, recede, withdraw, repel, repulse
mnemonic: An axe 斤 waves a hand 手 to push away — to REJECT and REPEL, pushing back with force.
Rice stalks 禾 scattered so far apart you can barely see them — RARE and PHENOMENAL, as DILUTE as a few grains in a vast field.
Grass 艹 rising above the water like a great peak 夫 — the LOTUS flower, noble and pure as Mount Fuji.
Words 言 descending from above 召 like a call from heaven — the IMPERIAL EDICT spoken by the emperor himself.
The fifth month 皐 when swamps fill with summer rain — the SHORE of a SWAMP blooming in early summer.
A tiny bird 隹 sheltered by tall grass 刍 and feathers 隹 — the CHICK or DUCKLING, a soft little doll of a bird.

kunyomi: おも.んみる, これ, おも.うに
mnemonic: A heart 忄 that keeps thinking 隹 — to CONSIDER deeply, to REFLECT and THINK with a still, focused mind.
A person 亻 lends their right hand 右 — to HELP and ASSIST someone in need.
Feathers 羽 and wings lit by blazing light 光 — to SHINE, SPARKLE, and GLEAM like a bird catching sunlight.
Eyebrows drawn in ink BLACK as charcoal 黒 — the BLACKENED EYEBROWS of a classical beauty.
Water 氵 soaks deeply and warmly 握 into the earth — a KINDNESS that MOISTENS and nourishes like gentle rain.

meaning: yearn after, long for, aspire to, admire, adore
onyomi: ショウ, トウ, ドウ
mnemonic: A heart 忄 that moves toward a distant light 童 — to YEARN AFTER and LONG FOR something you can barely see.
Under the roof 宀, as the day 肖 fades — the WEE HOURS of EVENING when the household grows quiet.
A woman 女 rising above 亡 all reason — DELUSION and RECKLESS thinking, acting WITHOUT AUTHORITY.

onyomi: シュン, ジュン, トン
mnemonic: A heart 忄 that is rich 享 and warm — SINCERE and KIND, treating others with genuine consideration.

kunyomi: おさ.める, なが.い, ほじし
mnemonic: Dried meat 月 (flesh) preserved long 攸 — DRIED MEAT, preserved for the journey ahead.

meaning: for the first time, not until
mnemonic: The first sprout pushing up from the earth — FOR THE FIRST TIME, the seed has become something new.

meaning: bar-tending, serving sake, the host, draw (water), ladle, scoop, pump
mnemonic: Lifting a ladle to pour sake from a crock 酉 — BAR-TENDING, SERVING SAKE, drawing water with a graceful scoop.
A worm 虫 hidden inside heaven 天 — the SILKWORM, divine creature that spins threads of silk from its own body.

kunyomi: うれ.しい, たの.しむ
mnemonic: A woman 女 lit up by joy 喜 — GLAD and PLEASED, bursting with delight.
Grass 艹 that has turned the deep BLUE of the sea — PALE and BLUE, the color of faded summer fields.
The sun 日 blazes with radiant light 軍 — to SHINE brilliantly, sending LIGHT across the landscape.
A head 頁 being divided 分 — to DISTRIBUTE and DISSEMINATE, splitting resources among the group.
A mouth 口 open and empty 八 — meaning ONLY this, and nothing more; FREE and ADDITIONAL.
The body's branches 月 that hold the support 支 — the LIMBS, the ARMS and LEGS of the body.
A tall tree 木 beside an altar 亶 — CEDAR or SANDALWOOD, the sacred wood of temples.

kunyomi: かちどき, やわらぐ
mnemonic: The wind 几 returns from VICTORY 豈 — the VICTORY SONG carried home on a triumphant breeze.

onyomi: スイ, エ, ケイ, セイ
mnemonic: A hand sweeping a broom 彐 through the sky — the COMET blazing through the heavens like a BROOM sweeping stars.
Words 言 copied from one scroll to another 膛 — to MIMEOGRAPH and COPY, duplicating documents faithfully.
A tree 木 remembered for its carved 辛 woodblocks — the CATALPA TREE, used for WOODBLOCK PRINTING since ancient times.

meaning: sign of the ox or cow, 1-3AM, second sign of Chinese zodiac
mnemonic: The shape of a hand twisted at an awkward hour — the SIGN OF THE OX, governing the dark hours of 1–3 AM.
A mouth 口 and ears surrounded by a gate 冊 — the HEIR who will SUCCEED to carry the family name forward.
A mouth 口 plus ten 十 voices speaking together — to GRANT and ANSWER, making a wish come true.

kunyomi: しお, うしお, せい
mnemonic: Water 氵 at the moment it shifts 夕 (evening) — the EVENTIDE TIDE, the pull of salt water at dusk.
Silk threads 糸 woven into a shining pattern 旬 — a BRILLIANT FABRIC DESIGN, dazzling in its intricacy.

meaning: conjunction (astronomy), first day of month, north
mnemonic: The moon 月 retreating back 屰 to zero — the CONJUNCTION, the FIRST DAY OF THE MONTH when the moon disappears.

onyomi: カ, ガ, キャ, ギャ
mnemonic: A person 亻 who tends to others like a healer 加 — NURSING and ATTENDING, the caring entertainer at your side.

onyomi: ボウ, ホ, モ, ム
mnemonic: A field 田 with ribs running through it 久 — the FURROW and RIDGE of a plowed field, thirty tsubo of earth.

meaning: extract, selection, summary, copy, spread thin
mnemonic: A hand 扌 that skims and lifts 少 — to EXTRACT and COPY, spreading a thin selection across paper.

kunyomi: あき.らか, さわ.やか, たがう
mnemonic: Bound with cloth on both sides 大 with double marks — REFRESHING and BRACING, as clear and sweet as mountain air.
Black 黒 rising at the edge of dawn — DARK and many, the deep shade just before the first light breaks.
A lazy heart 忄 that lets everything slide 隋 — LAZINESS, surrendering to the pull of inaction.
A twisted insect 虫 buried beneath the earth 亦 — the BARBARIAN from beyond the borders, untamed and wild.

kunyomi: さ.える, こお.る, ひ.える
mnemonic: Ice 冫 sharpens the mind until it is perfectly CLEAR — SERENE and COLD, skilfully transparent.
The sun 日 blazes KING 王 — FLOURISHING and VIGOROUS, glowing with royal success.

meaning: show symptoms of, sprout, bud, malt
kunyomi: も.える, きざ.す, めばえ, きざ.し
mnemonic: Grass 艹 and a friend 朋 pushing through soil together — to SPROUT and BUD, showing the first SYMPTOMS of life.
A person 亻 with a sharp mind 思 that reaches back — to RECOLLECT and REMEMBER cherished things.
A single stroke wrapped in a ceremonial shell 壱 — the formal ONE used in official DOCUMENTS, secure against forgery.
A jewel 王 with the color of flowing streams 留 — LAPIS LAZULI, the deep blue gem kept like still water.

kunyomi: じょう, まこと.に, ゆるす
mnemonic: A person bowing in sincere agreement 儿 — to LICENSE and PERMIT with genuine SINCERITY.
A person 亻 with an arrow 矢 aimed at the target — the MARQUIS or LORD, the one who shoots true.
Grass seeds 艹 placed carefully into time 時 — to SOW SEEDS, planting them at the right moment.
A fish 魚 that profits 利 from its persistence — the CARP, fighting upstream through every current.
A bow 弓 curved like a melon 瓜 — the ARC and ARCH, the perfect curving shape of a drawn bow.
Moving along 辶 a path that shakes 揺 far away — FAR OFF and DISTANT, a long road stretching into the past.

kunyomi: かね, かねざし, さしがね
mnemonic: A carpenter's tool 矢 shaped like a corner 巨 — the RULER and CARPENTER'S SQUARE, measuring perfect right angles.

meaning: type of morning glory, rose of Sharon, althea
mnemonic: A flowering vine twisting heavenward 舛 — the ROSE OF SHARON blooming at dawn, fleeting and beautiful.
A gem 王 that reflects light like a central point 英 — the SPARKLE OF JEWELRY, pure as CRYSTAL.
A person 阝 who attaches 付 themselves — to AFFIX and ATTACH, to append a note to the letter.
A tiger 彡 with brilliant spots 虎 — SPOTTED and PATTERNED, the small tiger wearing a coat of vivid marks.

meaning: sign of the hare or rabbit, fourth sign of Chinese zodiac, 5-7AM, east
mnemonic: The gate 門 thrown open at dawn — the SIGN OF THE HARE, when the east brightens at 5–7 AM.
A person 亻 who stands firm with a single word 旦 — marking the HOWEVER that turns the sentence around.
A silk 糸 cloth woven in a perfect pattern 奇 — FIGURED CLOTH, strikingly BEAUTIFUL in its design.
Grass 艹 whose root 于 swells underground — the TARO or YAM, the tuber hiding in the dark soil.
Grass 艹 with a western 西 tint at sunset — MADDER plant producing a deep RED DYE, the color of dusk.

meaning: endure, keep (rain)out, stave off, tide over, defy, slight, surpass
mnemonic: Ice 冫 that must be endured when the water 夌 rises — to ENDURE and STAVE OFF, defying the cold flood.
A white 白 shining face 告 — pure WHITE and CLEAR, glowing like moonlight.
Water 氵 that sparkles in the light 光 — SPARKLING WATER dancing in sunbeams.
A ball made with a spiny shell 求 — a BURR or BALL, round and covered in tiny prickles.
A wave 波 of a woman 女 — the OLD WOMAN, as weathered and persistent as a rolling wave.
Silk 糸 dyed the color of a cascade 皮 — SCARLET and CARDINAL, the vivid red of a warrior's cord.
A fish 魚 prized at the dinner table 周 — the SEA BREAM, the RED SNAPPER, king of celebratory meals.

onyomi: レイ, レン, リョウ
mnemonic: A heart 忄 that commands 令 sharply — WISE and perceptive, with a clear compassionate intelligence.

meaning: village, rural community, right village radical (no. 163)
mnemonic: A boundary 囗 with a kneeling person 巴 below — the VILLAGE and RURAL COMMUNITY, a bounded place of belonging.
A person 亻 who looks to the side 放 — to EMULATE and IMITATE, copying what you admire.
A stone 石 with the color of a king's 王 jade — BLUE and GREEN, the color of turquoise waters.

onyomi: タク, ツク, トク
mnemonic: A mouth 口 tapping like a hammer 豕 — to PECK and PICK UP, like a woodpecker drilling into bark.
Grain 禾 overflowing in GREAT abundance 襄 — GOOD CROPS and PROSPERITY, a harvest beyond measure.

meaning: west, bird, sign of the bird, 5-7PM, tenth sign of Chinese zodiac, sake radical (no. 164)
mnemonic: A sake jug filled to the shoulder — the SIGN OF THE BIRD, the tenth zodiac sign, ruling 5–7 PM.
A heart 忄 that steps aside for elders 弟 — SERVING OUR ELDERS, the respectful younger sibling.

kunyomi: つま.しい, つづまやか
mnemonic: A person 亻 who checks 検 their spending twice — FRUGAL and THRIFTY, living with careful ECONOMY.
